purpose: Reusable motion catalog for the Remotion video pipeline. Agents must choose from these presets instead of inventing one-off animation styles.

beatContract.rule: Keep frame/action for the current renderer. Use preset/anchor so the next renderer can bind motion to caption timing.

presets.description: Small breathing scale and screen glow for DouDou robot. Use as background life, not as the main emphasis.
Gentle scale-in for one object or label. Use once per shot for the main idea.
Short slide with opacity for cards, tablets, or rules. Keep travel under 48px.
A restrained highlight flash on a tablet, badge, or warning item. Avoid full-screen flashes.
Draws a check, underline, or rule line. Good for rules-board and verification scenes.
Magnifier or scan line moves across answer/source content. Use for verify-answer only.
Subtle outline pulse around a boundary, rule, or safe area. Stronger than robot-breathe.
Question bubble appears with a tiny vertical bob. Use for ask-the-purpose scenes.
Warm glow around a bulb or idea card. Use to mark a useful parent action.
Tiny timer tick or hourglass nudge. Use once when talking about limits or enough.
A short warning-card nudge. Use sparingly for risk, wrong answer, or trap.
Draws an orange underline or wave under the active keyword. Bind to anchor=keyword.

shotRules: Use at most one medium/high emphasis per shot.
Bind keyword-underline or the strongest preset to anchor=keyword.
Keep motion durations between 8 and 36 frames.
Prefer low or medium intensity. High intensity is only for the hook or final warning.
Do not animate every object at the same time.
